text.title: lang-ruby-sorbet-strict-floor Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for lang-ruby-sorbet-strict-floor. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every Ruby file under directories writable by an AI agent MUST start with # typed: true or # typed: strict. CI MUST run bundle exec srb tc and bundle exec tapioca check-shims; both must exit 0 before merge. Agents are forbidden from downgrading a file's sigil to silence errors; they must fix the type instead.
rule.rationale: Sorbet at typed: true rejects calls to methods Sorbet does not see in any RBI, which is exactly the failure mode of LLM-invented methods. Without srb tc in CI, the typecheck is advisory and agents will route around it (per Shopify's adoption write-up).

rule.statement: Every project ships bin/tapioca; the workflow is tapioca init once, then tapioca gem after Gemfile.lock changes, tapioca dsl after model/concern changes. RBIs (sorbet/rbi/gems/*.rbi, sorbet/rbi/dsl/*.rbi) MUST be committed. CI MUST run bundle exec tapioca check-shims AND bundle exec tapioca dsl --verify — any divergence fails the build.
rule.rationale: Without check-shims and dsl --verify, an agent can edit code that invalidates a checked-in RBI, get a green local typecheck against the stale RBI, and ship a method call that breaks at boot. The verify pass is the determinism guarantee.
